metadata.description: A physical machine as a managed ASSET (the box itself) — the raw-resource form of compute. It can exist unallocated in inventory (lifecycle_state: available, §28), carries the reconciled Hardware.* component rollup (§26), and is the thing that hosts workloads (VMs, containers, OSDs). An ALLOCATION of a host to a consumer is NOT a separate type — it is the Ownership/Allocation model (foundations/ownership-sharing-allocation.md §2.1 whole-allocation); a running 'instance' is a realized entity of this type. Grounded in Redfish ComputerSystem (inventory/system) + Metal3 BareMetalHost (provisioning lifecycle).

lifecycle_state.description: Allocation state (common-elements §6, §28). available = inventoried/unallocated raw asset; allocated = adopted into the managed lifecycle. Adopts Metal3 BareMetalHost.status.provisioning.state.

composition_visibility.description: Whether the Hardware.* components are modeled as first-class entities (§26, entities/service-dependencies.md §11d). opaque = rollup only.

cpu.description: Reconciled CPU rollup (§26) — aggregate of contained Hardware.Processor when modeled. Adopts Redfish ProcessorSummary.

memory.description: Reconciled memory rollup (§26) — aggregate of contained Hardware.MemoryModule. Adopts Redfish MemorySummary.

storage.description: Reconciled local-storage rollup (§26) — aggregate of contained Hardware.StorageDevice.

power_state.description: Observed power state — On | Off | PoweringOn | PoweringOff (Redfish ComputerSystem.PowerState). Drives shutdown/startup ordering for the dependency graph.

management_address.description: Reachable management endpoint (BMC/Redfish or host management IP) once Realized.

relationships.description: Power source(s) the host draws from; the host stops before the feed in a reverse-topo shutdown.

adopts.role: physical system inventory + power vocabulary (identity, processor/memory summary, PowerState)

aliases.name: Dedicated Host
aliases.note: the physical-host asset; tenant instances run on it
